current.summary: Pause skill.md spec implementation to align terminology (platforms vs providers vs harnesses) across specs before writing fields

activeWork.description: Implementing skill.md front matter specification from agent ecosystem; discovered terminology inconsistency blocking spec work
activeWork.progress: Identified: agent ecosystem spec uses 'supported agent platforms', syllago codebase uses 'providers', other tools use 'harnesses'. This terminology must be locked down before implementing spec fields to avoid baking inconsistent naming into code. Agent ecosystem spec is meant to be community source of truth, so vocabulary alignment is prerequisite.

git.lastCommit: 91ccb23 feat(converter): align hook data layer with spec v0.1.0

recentDecisions: Pause skill.md spec implementation until terminology is aligned
Terminology must be consistent across agent ecosystem spec, syllago, and other tools before spec is finalized
nextSteps: Research terminology usage: agent ecosystem spec definition, syllago provider naming, other tools (harnesses?)
Decide on canonical term (platforms, providers, or harnesses)
Update skill.md spec with aligned vocabulary
Resume implementation of supported platforms field

context.notes: This is blocking the skill.md spec implementation because the spec will define the canonical naming going forward. Getting it wrong now creates tech debt across all implementations. Need to research across agent ecosystem org, syllago's provider concept, and how other tools (Claude Code, Cursor, Codeium, etc.) refer to this concept.
